objective: Make persistent context carryover real through canonical memory sources, stale-context rules, session handoff, and a working reference implementation.

canonical_sources.purpose: Codex memory, rollout summaries, prior decisions
Human-readable memory notes and operational references
Reference implementation — SQLite session memory with freshness tracking

bands.label: stale
bands.action: flag stale, do not act without re-validation

staleness_policy.default_retrieval: excludes is_stale=1; pass include_stale=True to override

store: non-secret cross-session facts (project paths, active branches, open decisions, blockers, contract versions)
batch execution state (which batch ran, passed/failed, next recommended)
session handoff records (changed files, validation results, stale assumptions)
reject: secret values, credentials, tokens, API keys, OAuth state
transient runtime state (PIDs, ports, temp readings)
large blobs > 4 KB (store pointer to source file instead)

known_gaps: No operational .rag_db was found at expected locations during the audit.
Some scheduler or knowledge-loop paths may still reference stale /home/caraxes paths.
Multiple memory systems exist without a single retrieval contract.

retrieval_validation.description: ingest non-secret note with memory_key, memory_value, source_path, scope
mark_stale_memory flags entries and sets stale_checked_at

completion_gate: A memory implementation can ingest and retrieve a non-secret note with source path and freshness metadata.
